figures.caption: Figure 1: Schematic of the radially symmetric single-wellbore GCS domain, including domain geometry, initial conditions, and boundary conditions.
Table 1: Summary of parameter space for input variables, sampling range and distribution. We refer to Wen et al. [34] for more detail on the construction of the dataset.
Table 2: Summary of data preprocessing applied to input and output variables.
Table 3: Architecture summary of the Temporal CNN surrogate model.
Table 4: Architecture summary of the U-Net surrogate model.
Table 5: Architecture summary of the V-Net surrogate model.
Figure 2: Fourier Neural Operator (FNO) architecture schematic.
Table 6: Architecture summary of the Fourier Neural Operator (FNO) surrogate model. The Fourier modes are set to kmax = 10 in all spatial and temporal dimensions, with a channel width of NW = 36.
Figure 3: U-Net Enhanced Fourier Neural Operator (U-FNO) architecture schematic.
Table 7: Architecture summary of the U-FNO surrogate model. The Fourier modes are set to kmax = 10 in all spatial and temporal dimensions, with a channel width of NW = 36.
Table 8: U-Net block used in Fourier Layers 4–6 of the U-FNO (Table 7).
Figure 4: Test dataset root mean square error (RMSE) vs. standard deviation in permeability field for all models for CO2 saturation field.
Figure 5: Input fields and ground truth CO2 saturation field for Test Case 1. Scalar inputs: Qinj = 1.99 MT/yr, T = 118.9 ◦C, Pinit = 213.3 bar, Swi = 0.24, λ = 0.52.
Figure 6: Input fields and ground truth CO2 saturation field for Test Case 2. Scalar inputs: Qinj = 0.66 MT/yr, T = 148.6 ◦C, Pinit = 279.4 bar, Swi = 0.15, λ = 0.45.

Figure 7: CO2 saturation field predictions for Test Case 1: (a) predicted field ŝ at t = 30.0 yr, (b) normalized absolute error |s− ŝ|/|s|max at t = 30.0 yr, and (c) parity plot over the full 30-year injection period.

Figure 8: CO2 saturation field predictions for Test Case 2: (a) predicted field ŝ at t = 30.0 yr, (b) normalized absolute error |s− ŝ|/|s|max at t = 30.0 yr, and (c) parity plot over the full 30-year injection period.
Figure 9: Power spectral density of the CO2 saturation field s at t = 30.0 yr for Test Case 1.
Figure 10: Power spectral density of the CO2 saturation field s at t = 30.0 yr for Test Case 2.
Table 10: Summary of model architecture and training cost for pressure build-up field.
Figure 11: Test dataset root mean square error (RMSE) vs. standard deviation in permeability field for all models for pressure build-up field.
Figure 12: Input fields and ground truth pressure build-up field (t = 30.0 yr) for Test Case 1. Scalar inputs: Qinj = 0.49 MT/yr, T = 87.9 ◦C, Pinit = 159.0 bar, Swi = 0.12, λ = 0.41.
Figure 13: Input fields and ground truth pressure build-up field (t = 30.0 yr) for Test Case 2. Scalar inputs: Qinj = 0.21 MT/yr, T = 57.0 ◦C, Pinit = 156.4 bar, Swi = 0.21, λ = 0.64.

Figure 14: Pressure build-up field predictions for Test Case 1: (a) predicted field p̂ at t = 30.0 yr, (b) normalized absolute error |p− p̂|/|p|max at t = 30.0 yr, and (c) parity plot over the full 30-year injection period.

Figure 15: Pressure build-up field predictions for Test Case 2: (a) predicted field p̂ at t = 30.0 yr, (b) normalized absolute error |p− p̂|/|p|max at t = 30.0 yr, and (c) parity plot over the full 30-year injection period.
Figure 16: Power spectral density of the pressure build-up field p at t = 30.0 yr for Test Case 1.
Figure 17: Power spectral density of the pressure build-up field p at t = 30.0 yr for Test Case 2.
Table A.11: Model performance results for CO2 saturation field – radial gradient loss (λz = 0).
Table A.12: Model performance results for CO2 saturation field – full gradient loss (λr = λz).
Table A.13: Model performance results for pressure build-up field – radial gradient loss (λz = 0).
Table A.14: Model performance results for pressure build-up field – full gradient loss (λr = λz).
